title: Strolid Leadership Call
summary: The Strolid leadership team discussed the profound impact of AI and LLMs on the automotive retail industry. Vinnie Micciche presented a vision for Strolid to transition from a BDC to an AI company, leveraging existing infrastructure to become a 'middleware' or 'trust layer' connecting LLMs with dealers. Key topics included the future of website traffic, digital retailing tools, and the need for a seamless customer journey from LLMs to dealerships. The team agreed on piloting new AI-driven chat widgets and focusing on building functional AI applications.

decisions.description: Sponsor VCON dinner and secure the VCON Foundation meeting room.
Start closely monitoring and watching expenses after the VCON show.
Prioritize raising money for VCON to support the Strolid business.
Adopt an 'agents' approach for AI product development, focusing on specific functionalities.
Proceed with the plan to start testing a chat widget on dealer websites.

actionItems.task: Talk to Perry about the need to watch expenses closely.

expectedOutcome.description: Perry is aware of the need for tighter expense control.

actionItems.task: Frame the new company vision and strategy to the internal team.

expectedOutcome.description: Team understands the strategic shift towards AI and new product direction.

actionItems.task: Identify and prioritize the shortest path for early AI product wins.

expectedOutcome.description: Clear roadmap for initial AI feature development and deployment.

actionItems.task: Build AI tools one at a time, starting with inventory, then appointment scheduling and payments.

expectedOutcome.description: Modular AI applications integrated into the platform.

tensions: Vinnie expressed concern about serious BDC churn problems and impending cash flow issues.
Vinnie highlighted the potential elimination of website traffic and existing digital retailing tools by LLMs.
Vinnie noted the current disconnection in the customer journey from LLMs to dealers, leading to frustration.
Michael Donovan questioned the data source for Strolid acting as a 'trust layer' for LLMs.
Michael Donovan raised concerns about historically low form conversion rates on automotive websites.
referencesToPast: Vinnie mentioned almost building a digital retailing tool in 2019.
Vinnie recalled almost building a chat platform when talking to Ford and Toyota.
Vinnie referred to previous focus on infrastructure like VCON and Conserver.
Vinnie mentioned David Rosenberg's past description of Strolid GPT as a 'dealer MRI'.
Vinnie referenced Thomas Howe's prior discussions about 'co-pilots'.
Vinnie mentioned his past opposition to digital retailing tools.
Vinnie referred to discussions with Kevin about unlocking data within conversations.
Vinnie recalled David Rosenberg's process around Darwin in his showroom.
Vinnie mentioned Perry's ongoing assertion that Strolid is an AI company.

contributions.description: Presented the idea that LLMs like Strolid GPT can answer complex customer questions.
Proposed the concept of LLMs evolving into marketplaces, diminishing website traffic.
Introduced the vision of Strolid becoming an 'AIBDC' with 'Dealer Co-Pilot' and 'Dealer Brain'.
Suggested Strolid could act as middleware, connecting LLMs to dealers securely.
Advocated for building 'agents' that work together for AI product development.
Suggested focusing on the shortest path for early AI wins.
Highlighted the advantage of a full end-to-end closed-loop conversational stack.
Identified quick wins, faster response times, better leads, and a bigger TAM as benefits.
Recommended piloting the new approach with a small number of dealers for feedback.
Provided details on VCON show dates, locations, and sponsorship costs.
Shared information about the IETF conference in China being significantly cheaper.
Explained LLMs as part of an infrastructure feeding into functional AI applications.
Suggested mapping the buyer journey to identify areas for AI application.
Stated Strolid's strength is access to inventory data for dealerships.
Proposed building AI tools one at a time, starting with inventory, then scheduling.
